summary: A polished, music-driven montage of the unveiling of two enormous 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST' banners (one with Trump's portrait, one with a historical president's portrait) draped down the facade of the Department of Labor building beside a giant American flag, while a crowd films on phones. Slow cinematic camera moves and color grading build a triumphant, patriotic spectacle celebrating the administration's worker-first branding. The stylized cinematic treatment, music bed, and DOL seal end card make it produced promotional content rather than a raw event recording.

script.scene: Crowd in suits seen from behind filming on phones; two huge vertical 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST' banners (Trump portrait left, historical president portrait right) hang on the DOL building with US flags.
Wider hero shot: a massive American flag unfurls down the center of the facade between the two banners; crowd watches and records.
Symmetrical composition of the full facade - flag centered, 'FIRST' banners flanking, blue-sky daylight, slight cinematic grade.
Camera tilts to crowd silhouettes in foreground against the banner-draped building, dusk/dramatic toning.
Cut to black end card: white 'Department of Labor / United States of America' seal.

notable_text: DEPARTMENT OF LABOR UNITED STATES OF AMERICA
